summary: Conjecture 1.6 from arXiv:2204.12683 posits fractional 19/7-colorability for subcubic triangle-free graphs avoiding four specific forbidden subgraphs, serving as an intermediate step between the paper's main result (11/4-colorability avoiding two graphs) and the stronger Conjecture 1.3 (8/3-colorability avoiding six graphs). No follow-up work addressing this specific intermediate conjecture was found in five web searches spanning arXiv and related literature through May 2026. The conjecture remains open.
search_queries: subcubic triangle-free "19/7" fractional chromatic number Dvořák Lidický Postle
arxiv 2204.12683 subcubic triangle-free fractional colorability conjecture
subcubic triangle-free "fractional chromatic" "19/7" F11 F22 forbidden subgraph 2025 2026
Dvořák Lidický Postle "8/3" subcubic triangle-free fractional coloring 2025 2026 follow-up
notes: No follow-up found after 5 web calls. The conjecture is posed as an intermediate result between Theorem 1.4 (11/4-colorability, two forbidden graphs) and Conjecture 1.3 (8/3-colorability, six forbidden graphs). The paper itself was posted to arXiv in April 2022 and published in Advances in Combinatorics in 2025; the conjecture is at most ~4 years old with no known resolution.
